name: Spiced Pears in Cider
recipeIngredient: 1 pint Dry cider, strong or red wine
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 250°F / 130°C.
Peel the pears neatly, leaving the stalks on, and stand them in a large casserole.
In a saucepan bring the cider (or red wine), sugar, split vanilla pod, and cinnamon sticks to a boil.
Pour this mixture over the pears, cover the casserole, and bake very slowly for about 3 hours, turning the pears halfway through the cooking time.
When the pears are cooked, transfer them to a serving bowl and cool.
Pour the liquid into a saucepan, discarding the vanilla pod and cinnamon sticks.
Mix the corn starch (or arrowroot) with a little cold water to form a smooth paste, then add it to the saucepan with the remaining liquid. Bring to a boil, stirring until the mixture has thickened slightly to a syrup.
Pour this over the pears, allow to cool slightly, and baste each pear with a good coating of the syrup.
Place in the refrigerator to chill thoroughly and serve sprinkled with toasted flaked almonds and whipped cream.
